items.title: USDA seeks public comments on SNAP quality-control form; deadline July 31, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service published a Paperwork Reduction Act notice to reinstate and revise Form FNS-245 (Negative Case Action Review Schedule) used for SNAP quality control reviews. Comments are due by July 31, 2026, and FNS estimates total reporting/recordkeeping burden of about 121,597 hours, up from about 112,491.43 hours (an increase of about 9,105 hours).

items.title: June 2026 Social Security and SSI payment dates: June 1, 10, 17, and 24
items.summary: SSA’s 2026–2027 payment calendar lists SSI payments for June 2026 on June 1, 2026. For Social Security (RSDI), payments are scheduled for June 10 (birthdays 1–10), June 17 (11–20), and June 24 (21–31), with a separate note that people who got Social Security before May 1997 (or get both SSI and Social Security) are generally paid on the 3rd.

items.title: KFF tracker: Medicaid/CHIP enrollment about 74.9 million as of Feb. 2026
items.summary: KFF reports nationwide Medicaid/CHIP enrollment at about 74.9 million people as of February 2026, including 67.7 million enrolled in Medicaid and 7.2 million in CHIP. KFF notes this is a 21% decline from March 2023 enrollment, but still 5% higher than February 2020.

items.title: IRS: 2026 standard deduction amounts listed at $32,200 (joint) and $16,100 (single)
items.summary: In its overview of provisions for individuals and workers, the IRS lists 2026 standard deduction amounts of $32,200 for married couples filing jointly, $16,100 for single filers (and married filing separately), and $24,150 for heads of household. The same IRS page also lists 2026 AMT exemption amounts of $90,100 (single) and $140,200 (joint), and an estate tax basic exclusion amount of $15,000,000 for 2026 decedents.

items.title: IRS Free File is generally available Jan. 15 through Oct. 15; income cap cited at $89,000
items.summary: The IRS Taxpayer Advocate Service says IRS Free File is generally available each year from January 15 through October 15. For 2025 tax returns, TAS cites an income threshold of below $89,000 to use Free File guided software.

items.title: WIC program reminder: WIC serves pregnant/postpartum people and young children (up to age 5)
items.summary: USDA’s WIC program page describes WIC as providing supplemental foods, health care referrals, and nutrition education for low-income pregnant, breastfeeding, and postpartum people, plus infants and children up to age 5. Households can use the WIC state agency directory to find eligibility and application details.

items.title: SNAP reminder: applications are handled by states; check your local office for benefit dates
items.summary: USDA reminds applicants that SNAP applications are handled by state and local offices rather than USDA, and current participants should use their state’s SNAP information page for benefit availability dates and EBT customer service numbers. This is a good time to confirm your contact information with your SNAP office so you don’t miss renewal notices.
